title: Diosma aulonophila
intro: Diosma aulonophila, also known as the African bushveld or mountain buchu, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Diosma aulonophila contains bioactive constituents such as diosmetin, linalool, and ursolic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Diosma aulonophila include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as essential oils used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and relieve stress.

Diosma aulonophila is a species of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of South Africa.
